rationale: Both correctly land on the ground-truth disposition ('duplicate of #4808'), but X fabricates unverifiable specifics (a '9 tests pass on main' claim, a grep 'should return nothing', a full regression-test list) to assert the fix is already merged on main and tells users to just git pull — a confident claim that Y directly contradicts with a concrete, grounded observation (#4808 is CLOSED with mergedAt null, i.e. never merged). Y is better calibrated: it honestly flags that it can't verify upstream main state, cites real file/line evidence (pipeline_hunyuan_image3.py:458-462, transformer.py:2125/2343, test file lines 15-20), and surfaces an actionable, well-grounded finding (test coverage gap in _STALE_API_FILES) that materially helps close the loop. Y's hedged-but-substantive answer on a genuinely uncertain point (is the fix actually merged?) is more trustworthy and complete than X's confident but likely fabricated closure.
